decision_description: Phase 2 concept alignment confirmed for MAESTRO against topic HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02. Topic mappings for EO-01 (significant improvements in autonomy, robustness, and reliability through advanced planning, memory management, and reasoning) and EO-02 (innovative multi-agent frameworks demonstrating effective decentralised coordination beyond individual agent capability) are confirmed with direct Tier 3 evidence in concept_note.md, project_summary.json, and strategic_positioning.md. All 12 scope requirements (SR-01 through SR-12) and all 13 call constraints (CC-01 through CC-13) assessed: 9 SR elements fully covered; SR-12 partially covered (no explicit prior funded actions cited); CC-08, CC-09 partially covered (full partner country and control-structure enumeration deferred to Tier 3 consortium data); CC-10 partially covered (MAESTRO does not provide a full demonstrator for in-vehicle autonomous driving, substituting an architectural mapping analysis in WP8); CC-11 partially covered (no prior EU-funded actions identified for reuse); CC-13 partially covered (FSTP intent not determined). Three scope boundary flags raised as unresolved and carried forward: (1) logistics and supply chain demonstrator is not among the three Apply AI sectors named in SR-07 or CC-10 — inclusion is framed as cross-sector transfer, not as an Apply AI sector claim, but ambiguity must be resolved in Phase 3 WP design; (2) absence of a full in-vehicle autonomous driving demonstrator creates portfolio exposure under CC-10 that cannot be resolved by an architectural mapping analysis alone; (3) SME enablement and strategic dependency reduction framing in strategic_positioning.md has no confirming Tier 2B scope entry and must not be advanced as a scope-grounded claim in Tier 5 deliverables without Tier 2B authority. Concept vocabulary is aligned with call-specific expected outcomes and evaluation priorities. Strategic differentiation documented and grounded in Tier 3 concept_note.md architectural choices. Phase 2 gate condition assessed as met for confirmed and covered elements; partial coverage items and flags are logged as unresolved and must be resolved before Phase 5 impact architecture and Phase 8 drafting finalise content touching these boundary conditions.
alternatives_considered: Accept all scope claims including logistics, SME enablement, and strategic dependency reduction framing as in-scope without flagging: rejected — CLAUDE.md §13.2 prohibits inventing or inferring call constraints not present in Tier 2B; scope boundary is defined exclusively by Tier 2B source documents.

Flag the logistics demonstrator as out-of-scope and require its removal from the concept: held open — the logistics demonstrator does not claim to be an Apply AI sector demonstrator in its own right; it is explicitly framed as a cross-sector transfer demonstrator confirming generalisability. The minimum SR-07 requirement is met by healthcare and advanced manufacturing. Removal is not mandated by Tier 2B, but the boundary ambiguity must be resolved in Phase 3 WP design and Phase 5 impact architecture before finalisation.

Treat WP8 architectural mapping analysis as functionally equivalent to an in-vehicle autonomous driving demonstrator for CC-10 portfolio purposes: rejected — CC-10 specifies portfolio coverage by sector with demonstrated application; an architectural transferability analysis is not evidentially equivalent to a validated sector demonstrator under the CC-10 procedure exception text.

Declare Phase 2 gate condition failed due to unresolved flags: rejected — the three flags are scope boundary conditions where Tier 3 content extends beyond what Tier 2B explicitly endorses, not contradictions between the concept and Tier 2B. The Phase 2 gate condition requires absence of unresolved scope conflicts, which is met; the flags are logged for resolution in downstream phases per CLAUDE.md §12.2 and §12.3.
tier_authority_applied: Tier 2B scope_requirements.json (docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json); Tier 2B expected_outcomes.json (docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/expected_outcomes.json); Tier 2B call_constraints.json (docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json); CLAUDE.md §7 Phase 2 gate condition; CLAUDE.md §13.2 (prohibition on inventing call constraints not present in Tier 2B); CLAUDE.md §12.2 (validation status vocabulary — Confirmed, Inferred, Assumed, Unresolved); CLAUDE.md §12.3 (explicit conflict resolution recording requirement)
rationale: CLAUDE.md §7 Phase 2 gate condition requires: topic mapping confirmed; concept note vocabulary aligned with call-specific expected outcomes; strategic positioning identifying differentiation within call scope; no unresolved scope conflicts between the concept and Tier 2B. EO-01 and EO-02 mappings satisfy the topic mapping requirement with direct Tier 3 evidence chains to concept_note.md, project_summary.json, and strategic_positioning.md. Concept vocabulary reproduces call-specific terms including 'next-generation AI agents', 'planning mechanisms', 'memory management', 'multi-agent frameworks', 'decentralised coordination', and 'external tool and API integration'. Strategic differentiation is grounded in specific architectural choices (neuro-symbolic planning with formal guarantees, formally specified multi-agent delegation protocols with convergence guarantees, cross-cutting External Tool and API Orchestration Layer architecturally integrated across all three pillars) rather than generic positioning claims. The three scope flags do not constitute scope conflicts between the concept and Tier 2B — no Tier 2B requirement is contradicted by concept content; the flags identify where concept content either extends beyond Tier 2B endorsement (logistics framing, SME enablement claim) or falls short of a call-named criterion (in-vehicle autonomous driving demonstrator gap). Per CLAUDE.md §12.2, these are logged as Unresolved and must be resolved before Tier 5 content touching these elements can be finalised. Phase 2 gate is assessed as passable on confirmed and covered elements; CLAUDE.md §12.4 requires that missing mandatory inputs trigger gate failure, but the flagged items are not missing mandatory inputs — they are boundary ambiguities and a strategic portfolio gap. The unresolved flags are durable decision records carried into Phase 3 and Phase 5.
